Satbara Utara: Record of Land Rights (7/12 Extract) — a government document in Maharashtra recording land ownership, cultivation, and encumbrances. Essential for verifying clear title.
Visar Pavti: Earnest Money Receipt — an official receipt issued upon payment of a token/booking amount for a property, forming the basis of a preliminary sale agreement.
FSI: Floor Space Index — the ratio of a building's total floor area to the area of the plot on which it stands. Governed by DCPR (Development Control and Promotion Regulations).
TDR: Transferable Development Rights — a mechanism allowing construction rights to be transferred from a reserved plot to another plot in lieu of surrendering land for public purposes.
Mukhtyarnamaa: Power of Attorney — a legal document authorizing a person (agent) to act on behalf of the property owner for transactions, registration, or construction activities.
Chalu Sanad: Current Occupancy Certificate — a certificate issued by local authorities confirming that a building has been constructed as per the approved plan and is fit for occupation.
Namuna 7: Form 7 (Rights Register) — the ownership portion of the 7/12 extract showing the legal title holder of the land parcel.
Namuna 12: Form 12 (Possession Register) — the cultivation/possession portion of the 7/12 extract showing who is in actual possession of the land.
Encumbrance Certificate: A certificate from the Sub-Registrar's office confirming the property is free from legal dues, mortgages, or liens for a specified period.
NA Order: Non-Agricultural Order — a government order under the Maharashtra Land Revenue Code converting agricultural land to non-agricultural use for construction purposes. Mandatory for residential development.
IOD: Intimation of Disapproval — a document issued by the municipal authority granting conditional permission to commence construction after scrutiny of plans. Does not mean final approval.
CC: Commencement Certificate — formal permission issued by the municipal authority to a developer to begin construction after submitting the required documents and paying fees.
OC: Occupancy Certificate — issued upon completion of construction, certifying the building is fit for occupation as per approved plans. Required for legal possession.
RERA: Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 — Indian legislation regulating the real estate sector; in Maharashtra administered by MahaRERA.
MahaRERA: Maharashtra Real Estate Regulatory Authority — the state regulatory body under RERA that registers and monitors real estate projects and agents in Maharashtra.
Form 3: CA Certificate under MahaRERA — quarterly compliance report submitted by a Chartered Accountant disclosing funds collected from buyers, funds deposited in the designated escrow account, and funds withdrawn.
Form 1: Architect's Certificate under MahaRERA — quarterly certificate submitted by the project architect certifying the percentage of structural construction completed.
Escrow Account: Designated bank account under MahaRERA Section 4(2)(l)(D) where 70% of amounts collected from buyers must be deposited and withdrawn only for land and construction costs upon certification.
Carpet Area: The net usable floor area of an apartment excluding the thickness of external walls, areas under service shafts, exclusive balcony, or verandah area. Defined under RERA Section 2(k).
Conveyance Deed: The final legal document transferring ownership of property from the seller/developer to the buyer. Must be registered at the Sub-Registrar's office.
Deed of Declaration: A legal document executed by the promoter under the Maharashtra Ownership Flats Act (MOFA) declaring the details of the scheme and individual units for cooperative housing societies.
ULC: Urban Land Ceiling — relates to the Urban Land (Ceiling and Regulation) Act. Although repealed in Maharashtra in 2007, older properties may still carry ULC-related encumbrances.
Premium FSI: Additional floor space index purchased from the local municipal authority by paying a premium, over and above the base FSI permissible under DCPR.
Khasra Number: A survey number assigned to a plot of land in rural/semi-urban Maharashtra, used in revenue records to uniquely identify a parcel.
Gut Number: Unique plot/field identification number used in Maharashtra's land revenue records (synonymous with survey number for agricultural land).
Layout Approval: Permission granted by the planning authority for subdividing a larger land parcel into multiple plots with defined roads and open spaces as per DCPR.
BU Permission: Building Use Permission — another term for Occupancy Certificate in certain municipal jurisdictions in Maharashtra.
Hissa: A share or portion — refers to a sub-divided portion of a larger survey number, e.g., 'Hissa No. 2 of Survey No. 45'.
Pavti: Receipt — a general term for official payment receipts, often used in the context of property tax receipts (Gharkul Pavti) or local body charges.
Demand Draft: A negotiable instrument used for property payments, particularly for government stamp duty and registration charges.
Stamp Duty: A state government tax levied on property documents (sale deed, gift deed, etc.) at the time of registration. Rates in Maharashtra vary by property type and location.
